description: Dev-server launch configs for Master Control Orchestration Server. On the host machine, the primary admin UI is the Windows WinUI desktop shell (MasterControlShell.exe). The HTTP server on :7300 is the admin API + a browser admin surface intended for REMOTE access only (other machines on the LAN / trusted network). Configs below are ordered: host-primary first, remote-access second, dev-build variants last. Paths point at the staged-installed tree (C:/Program Files/Master Control Orchestration Server); change `runtimeExecutable` to build/release/src/<Module>/Release/<Module>.exe to run a fresh dev build without reinstalling.

configurations.name: MasterControl Shell (host primary UI)
configurations.notes: WinUI 3 desktop shell — the intended admin UI when you are sitting at the host. Statically links MasterControlApp.lib so it shares the same in-process runtime as the service host, and also polls /api/activity on localhost:7300 for the live command stream. Launch AFTER the service host is running.

configurations.name: MasterControl Service Host (SCM-managed)
configurations.notes: Production deployment topology: Windows service registered by MasterControlBootstrapper install, auto-starts on boot, LocalSystem context. Requires elevated PowerShell to start/stop/query. This is the companion backend for the Shell above.

configurations.name: MasterControl Service Host (console, foreground)
configurations.notes: Dev-only alternative: runs the installed service-host binary in foreground console mode, bypassing SCM. Useful for watching log output during debugging. Do NOT run this at the same time as the SCM-managed service — both would try to bind :7300.

configurations.name: MasterControl Browser Admin UI (remote access)
configurations.notes: REMOTE-ACCESS ONLY: the browser admin surface is intended for operators on other machines on the LAN who need to manage the orchestrator without installing the Shell. Replace HOST_MACHINE_IP with the host's LAN IP. The firewall rule 'MasterControl Browser Admin UI' opens :7300 for LAN inbound (created by bootstrapper install unless --skip-firewall). When you are at the host, use the Shell instead — it's the primary UI.

configurations.name: MasterControl Shell (dev build)
configurations.notes: Runs the freshly-built shell from build/release without reinstalling. Needs staged resources (share/MasterControlOrchestrationServer) accessible relative to the exe — run `cmake --install` into a staging dir first, or copy resources/ next to the dev-build exe.

configurations.name: MasterControl Service Host (dev build)
configurations.notes: Runs the freshly-built service host from build/release without reinstalling. Same resource-staging caveat as the dev-build Shell above. Stop the SCM-managed service first so both don't fight over :7300.
